title: Diagonal
description:
Implement `diag(a)`, which returns the main diagonal of a square matrix $a \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n}$:

$$\text{diag}(a)_k = a_{k,k}$$

**Input**: `a`, shape `[n, n]`. **Output**: shape `[n]`.

Solve with broadcasting/indexing only — build the diagonal with `torch.arange`-based fancy indexing, no python loop.
starter_code:
import torch

def diag(a):
    # Return the length-n main diagonal of square matrix a.
    pass
reference:
import torch

def diag(a):
    n = a.shape[0]
    out = torch.zeros(n, dtype=a.dtype)
    for k in range(n):
        out[k] = a[k, k]
    return out
hints: Solve in 1 line using broadcasting only.
Fancy-index a with the same arange vector for both the row and column axis: a[idx, idx].

solutions.name: Fancy indexing with arange
solutions.explanation: Indexing $a$ with `idx = torch.arange(n)` on both axes simultaneously selects $a_{k,k}$ for every $k$, i.e. exactly the main diagonal.
solutions.code:
import torch

def diag(a):
    idx = torch.arange(a.shape[0])
    return a[idx, idx]
